questions.question: 1 (a) Record the room temperature θ R shown on the thermometer in Fig. 2.1. θ R = ......................................................... [1] (b) The student pours a volume of 200 cm 3 of hot water into the beaker and records the temperature θ of the water at time t = 0. She records the temperature of the water in the beaker every 30 s.

She records the temperature of the water in the beaker every 30 s.

W B = 0.35 (N) V = 56 (cm ) line of sight perpendicula r / at eye level / at right angles V present and  B = 1.9(4) (g / cm ) suggestion supported by valid reason e.g.:   B as volume is measured directly   A as measuring cylinder is less precise 0625/63 May/June 2024
 R = 18 (°C) two suitable precautions e.g:  view scale reading perpendicularly  wait until readings stops rising  avoid thermometer touching beake r s, °C both correct

statement matching readings in table comparison of temperature changes over 180 s, matching statement x = 0.12 and x = 0.089 unit °C / s explanation matching values of x and x two from:  volume(s) of water  same / size / material / surface area of beaker  room temperature 0625/63 May/June 2024

move screen slowly / back and forth h O = 2(.0) and h I = 3.2 both to nearest mm W = 0.63  axes labelled with quantity and unit  appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid between plotted points)  plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots  well- j udged line and thin line G present and triangle method seen on graph f in range 14.0 (cm) to 17.5 (cm) and to 2 or 3 significant figures

values of h I become very small / difficult to measure or greater % uncertainty 0625/63 May/June 2024 4 MP1 apparatus: ammeter and voltmeter MP2 circuit diagram: correct circuit symbols shown in correct connection MP3 method: record diameter measure p.d and current repeat for new diameter MP4 control variable: any one from:  length of wire  material of wire  temperature of wire MP5 table: columns, with units, for diameter, p.d., current, (resistance) any variable mentioned must have correct units MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in diameter produces change in resistance plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point any one from:  at least 5 sets of data taken  repeat for each value of independent variable and take average  2nd appropriate control variable stated
